text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old woodpecker named Willow. Every morning, she'd peck at juicy berries, chirp happily, and listen to the songs of other creatures. She knew the forest like the back of her feathered head.

One autumn, a great wolf pack moved into the woods. The birds screamed, the squirrels hid their acorns, and even Willow felt uneasy. But she remembered her dear friend Oakleaf, the clever rabbit, who had always admired her wisdom.

One evening, as the stars twinkled outside her birdhouse sanctuary, Willow thought about Oakleaf. She imagined him saying, "Willow, I've found the smartest owl I know."

"How fascinating," Willow declared before drifting off to sleep. In her dreams, her brain spun fantastical tales of how Oakleaf deduced that autumn birds have fluffy tails, acorns grow underground, and owls always wear red noses.

When she woke up, the forest was calm again.
